---
title: "index"
format: html
---


Ich nutze **GraalPy 3.12.8 (Oracle GraalVM Native 25.0.1)**. Installiert über 

```batch
pyenv install graalpy-25.0.1 
```
und mittels

```batch
pyenv global graalpy-25.0.1
```
aktiviert.

### Vorbereitung mittels HomeBrew

Ich lade die folgenden Programme via HomeBrew:

- clang

- flang

- cmake

- bazel (für tensorflow)

- OpenBLAS (0.3.31_1.arm64_tahoe)


Vielleicht wichtig:
```
For compilers to find openblas you may need to set:
  export LDFLAGS="-L/opt/homebrew/opt/openblas/lib"
  export CPPFLAGS="-I/opt/homebrew/opt/openblas/include"

For pkgconf to find openblas you may need to set:
  export PKG_CONFIG_PATH="/opt/homebrew/opt/openblas/lib/pkgconfig"

For cmake to find openblas you may need to set:
  export CMAKE_PREFIX_PATH="/opt/homebrew/opt/openblas"
```

Daher:

```batch
brew install clang flang cmake
export CMAKE_PREFIX_PATH="/opt/homebrew/opt/openblas"
brew install openblas
```

### Installieren der Python-Pakete

- numpy 2.2.6
- pandas
- scilearn
- keras
  Dafür muss h5py installet werden, davor
  ```
  brew install hdf5
  export HDF5_DIR=$(brew --prefix hdf5)
  python -m pip install h5py
  ```
- PyTourch (2.7.0)
```batch
python -m pip pandas numpy scikit-learn tourch
```

- tensorflow (braucht bazel)

*Funktioniert nicht....*
